What one should do?

Well, the first and foremost thing is to ‘Plan’ properly before going on a trek. Planning involves from deciding the location, checking the weather conditions, collecting proper gears for the trek and a lot more things. 

Our team has one member who has done Basic mountaineering course, Advance mountaineering course, Search and Rescue course to name a few along with several other course. He has summit many of the major peaks and is a proud mountaineering. Contact Us, to know more about what one should do before going on a trek.

Things to avoid

We have experienced and heard a lot of epic stories of those who luckily survived and those who could have made it if they had proper gears and pre-check up (health, weather forecast etc.) done.

We don’t recommend you to go on a trek if:

  1. You’re unhealthy or suffering from a disease.
  2. Your BMI is wither low or high.
  3. Your age is above 65 years (exceptions in some cases).
  4. You’re a first timer and choosing a trek from difficult category.

Our team has one member who has done Basic mountaineering course, Advance mountaineering course, Search and Rescue course to name a few along with several other course. He has summit many of the major peaks and is a proud mountaineering. Contact Us, to know more about what one should do before going on a trek.
